CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF DEC-02-2015 CONTINUED TO APR-06-2016 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

DISCOVERY, 302, DEFENDANT SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, TO DEEM REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION ADMITTED, AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF SHALL SERVE CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSES WITHIN 15 DAYS TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, I; SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, I, REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION, I. IN ADDITION, THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION ARE DEEMED ADMITTED. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED IN THE AMOUNT OF $460 AND ARE PAYABLE TO DEFENDANT WITHIN 15 DAYS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: TOM COHEN, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-16-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. No opposition filed. Prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Defendant, Saint Francis Memorial Hospital?S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents, To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted, And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-13-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL'S Motion To Compel Responses To Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents, To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted, And For Monetary Sanctions is granted. Plaintiff shall serve code compliant responses within 15 days to Form Interrogatories, I; Special Interrogatories, I, Request for Production, I. In addition, the Requests for Admission are deemed admitted. Sanctions are awarded in the amount of $460 and are payable to Defendant within 15 days. ORDER SIGNED. Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/JPT)
